title: holodiscus discolor subsp. franciscanus
intro: Holodiscus discolor subsp. franciscanus, also known as the 'wild roses', is a plant species that belongs to the family Rosaceae. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Holodiscus discolor subsp. franciscanus include treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections.
